def policemen_catch_thieves(arr, k):
    n = len(arr)
    police_indices = []
    thief_indices = []

    for i in range(n):
        if arr[i] == 'P':
            police_indices.append(i)
        elif arr[i] == 'T':
            thief_indices.append(i)

    caught_thieves = 0
    p_ptr = 0  # Pointer for police_indices
    t_ptr = 0  # Pointer for thief_indices

    while p_ptr < len(police_indices) and t_ptr < len(thief_indices):
        p_idx = police_indices[p_ptr]
        t_idx = thief_indices[t_ptr]

        if abs(p_idx - t_idx) <= k:
            caught_thieves += 1
            p_ptr += 1
            t_ptr += 1
        elif p_idx < t_idx:
            # Current policeman is to the left of the current thief.
            # If they can't catch each other, this policeman cannot
            # catch this thief or any subsequent thief (further right).
            # Move to the next policeman.
            p_ptr += 1
        else:  # p_idx > t_idx
            # Current thief is to the left of the current policeman.
            # If they can't catch each other, this thief cannot
            # be caught by this policeman or any subsequent policeman (further right).
            # Move to the next thief.
            t_ptr += 1

    return caught_thieves
